title: Forward Dynamics
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Forward Dynamics: Forward dynamics: compute joint accelerations from applied torques and current state using Newton-Euler or Lagrangian mechanics. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.newton_euler.forward…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~80; inertia_parameter_uncertainty, friction_model_error set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: M(q)*q_ddot + C(q,q_dot)*q_dot + g(q) = tau; solve q_ddot = M^{-1}(tau - C*q_dot - g)
E.world_state_x: joint accelerations q_ddot(t) and integrated trajectory {q(t), q_dot(t)}
E.observation_y: applied joint torques tau(t) and joint states {q, q_dot}

W.regime: Existence of the recovered joint_acceleration_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 80); inertia_parameter_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: trajectory error < 1% of motion range for calibrated model. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~80. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |inertia_parameter_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(n) for ABA vs. O(n ** 3) for naive M inversion per iteration
